root_cause: The web service was deployed with RELEASE=v2, a bad release that failed readiness and returned HTTP 500 errors on /orders. The container itself stayed running and the payments service/database path was not implicated; logs from sreft-web explicitly reported the v2 readiness/orders regression and that v1 was the previous good release.
actions_taken: Inspected Docker container status, compose configuration, web/payments logs, and the running web environment. Confirmed /orders returned HTTP 500 while web was running with RELEASE=v2. Changed only the web service in docker-compose.yaml from RELEASE=v2 to RELEASE=v1, recreated only the web service with docker compose up -d web, and verified recovery with /healthz returning 200 and repeated /orders requests returning HTTP 200 with release v1. Left the payments and other unrelated containers untouched.
postmortem: A bad application release reached production without being caught before checkout traffic hit /orders, causing user-visible 500s while the process remained up. The incident was mitigated by a targeted rollback of the web service to the last known good release. Follow-up should add or strengthen pre-deploy checks for readiness and checkout/order-path behavior, improve automated rollback signals for red health checks plus elevated 5xx responses, and document the rollback runbook so responders can quickly change only the affected service during similar incidents.
